non_emergency_ambulance_calls

2026-04-25

R Markdown

Install and load in the libraries

rm(list = ls())
graphics.off()
library(magrittr) 
library(zoo)
## 
## Attaching package: 'zoo'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
library(forecast)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)

————————————————————–

Exploratory Data Analysis and Data Preparation

————————————————————–

data <- read.table("/Users/bratatipaul/Documents/DATA_SC_USW/DATA_MINING/Ambulance/ambulance_calls.txt", row.names = NULL, sep =",", header=TRUE)
data %>%
  head()
##   Year Month Calls
## 1 2013   Jan  2850
## 2 2013   Feb  2720
## 3 2013   Mar  2800
## 4 2013   Apr  2880
## 5 2013   May  3000
## 6 2013   Jun  3080
data %>%
  tail()
##     Year Month Calls
## 115 2022   Jul  3750
## 116 2022   Aug  3720
## 117 2022   Sep  3500
## 118 2022   Oct  3300
## 119 2022   Nov  3150
## 120 2022   Dec  3050
#order month as Jan 1, Feb 2 (not alphabatically)
data$Month <- factor(data$Month, levels = month.abb, ordered = TRUE)
data <- data[order(data$Year, data$Month), ]

—————————

Check for outliers (IQR)

—————————

Q1 <- quantile(data$Calls, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
Q3 <- quantile(data$Calls, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
IQR_val <- Q3 - Q1

upper_range <- Q3 + (1.5 * IQR_val)

outliers <- data[data$Calls > upper_range, ]

print(paste("Upper Outlier Threshold:", round(upper_range, 2)))
## [1] "Upper Outlier Threshold: 3700"
if (nrow(outliers) > 0) {
  print("Detected Outliers:")
  print(outliers)
} else {
  print("No outliers detected")
}
## [1] "Detected Outliers:"
##     Year Month Calls
## NA    NA  <NA>    NA
## 115 2022   Jul  3750
## 116 2022   Aug  3720
print("3750 and 3720 were identified as outliers but were retained as they are only marginally above the threshold.")
## [1] "3750 and 3720 were identified as outliers but were retained as they are only marginally above the threshold."
# Check duplicates
data[duplicated(data), ]
## [1] Year  Month Calls
## <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)
# Check missing values
sum(is.na(data))
## [1] 1
# Locate missing values
which(apply(data, 1, function(x) any(is.na(x))))
## 87 
## 87
# Inspect row with missing value
data[87, ]
##    Year Month Calls
## 87 2020   Mar    NA
# Handle missing value
data$Calls <- na.approx(data$Calls)
# Recheck missing values
sum(is.na(data$Calls))
## [1] 0

—————————

Create time series object

—————————

calls <- ts(data$Calls,
            start = c(data$Year[1], as.numeric(data$Month[1])),
            frequency = 12)

# average monthly non-emergency calls
avg <- mean(calls)
cat("Average call in each month = ", round(avg,2))
## Average call in each month =  3113.54
#plot raw series before any modelling
plot(calls,
     main = "Monthly non emergency calls",
     ylab = "calls", xlab = "Time")
abline(mean(calls), 0,
       col= 'red') #mean line over the plot

This series has clear upward trend and some seasonality is also present

# checking variance over different window
var_2013_17 <- var(window(calls, start=c(2013,1), end=c(2017,12)))
cat("variance of average response time in window of 2023-2017:", round(var_2013_17,2))
## variance of average response time in window of 2023-2017: 33245.06
var_2018_20 <- var(window(calls, start=c(2018,1), end=c(2020,12)))
cat("variance of average response time in window of 2018-2020:", round(var_2018_20,2))
## variance of average response time in window of 2018-2020: 45956.88
var_2021_22 <- var(window(calls, start=c(2021,1), end=c(2022,12)))
cat("variance of average response time in window of 2021-2022:", round(var_2021_22,2))
## variance of average response time in window of 2021-2022: 56478.08

#rolling variance grows over time.

# Apply logarithmic transformation to stabilize variance
log_calls <- log(calls)

# Plot the log-transformed series to inspect trend and seasonality
plot(log_calls, main = "non-emergency call(Log scaled)",
     ylab = "log(calls)", xlab = "Time")
abline(mean(log_calls), 0,
       col= 'red')

# Autocorrelation plots to check auto-correlation and stationarity
acf(log_calls, main = "ACF of original series(log scaled)", lag.max = 60)

pacf(log_calls, main = "PACF of original series(log scaled)", lag.max = 60)

Seasonality and Trend Assessment

# Seasonal pattern diagnostic

# Seasonal plot: shows the shape of the typical annual cycle.
seasonplot(log_calls, year.labels = TRUE,
           main = "Seasonal variation of Calls",
           ylab = "log(calls)", xlab = "Month")

# in seasonal plot there is pattern like high for July, August for each year 

# month plot: shows seasonal pattern across months
monthplot(log_calls, year.labels = TRUE,
           main = "Month wise variation of Calls",
           ylab = "log(calls)", xlab = "Month")
## Warning in plot.window(...): "year.labels" is not a graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy, type, ...): "year.labels" is not a graphical parameter
## Warning in box(...): "year.labels" is not a graphical parameter
## Warning in title(...): "year.labels" is not a graphical parameter
## Warning in axis(1, at = 1L:f, labels = labels, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in axis(2, ...): "year.labels" is not a graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter
## Warning in plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = type, ...): "year.labels" is not a
## graphical parameter

# The month plot shows a clear seasonal pattern similar to seasonal plot
# indicating a systematic, repeating monthly structure.

# Boxplots: show clear differences between months and their variability.
boxplot(log_calls ~ cycle(log_calls),
        main = "Monthly Variability of Ambulance Calls",
        xlab = "Month", ylab = "log(calls)")

# Quantify seasonality roughly
month_means <- tapply(log_calls, cycle(log_calls), mean)
seasonality_ratio <- var(month_means) / var(log_calls)
cat("Seasonality ratio : ", round(seasonality_ratio,2))
## Seasonality ratio :  0.77

The series exhibits strong seasonal patterns, with peaks in

July–August and variability across months, as shown by the monthplot,

seasonplot, and boxplots also confirmed by the seasonality ratio.

# Trend pattern diagnostic

# Trend inspection using rolling mean
trend_ma <- rollmean(log_calls, 12, fill=NA)
plot(trend_ma, main = "Rolling 12-Month Trend Mean", 
     xlab = "Time", ylab = "log_calls")

# rolling average plot shows a clear upward trend

# Quantify trend -- linear regression
summary(lm(calls ~ time(calls)))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = calls ~ time(calls))
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -304.72 -166.95  -37.36  142.98  441.29 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) -83602.889  12576.587  -6.648 9.73e-10 ***
## time(calls)     42.972      6.232   6.895 2.84e-10 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 197.1 on 118 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.2872, Adjusted R-squared:  0.2811 
## F-statistic: 47.54 on 1 and 118 DF,  p-value: 2.837e-10

Time Series Decomposition

# Seasonal-Trend decomposition using Loess (STL)

stl_calls <- stl(log_calls, s.window = "periodic", t.window = 13) 
# t.window = 13 means the trend is smoothed over a local window of 13 months.

# Plot decomposition to visually inspect components
plot(stl_calls, main = "STL Decomposition of non-emergency Ambulance Calls")

# Extract components 
seasonal_component <- stl_calls$time.series[, "seasonal"]
trend_component <- stl_calls$time.series[, "trend"]
remainder_component <- stl_calls$time.series[, "remainder"]

summary(seasonal_component)
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -0.08762 -0.04946 -0.01516  0.00000  0.04814  0.10361
summary(trend_component)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   7.979   8.008   8.036   8.041   8.072   8.111
summary(remainder_component)
##       Min.    1st Qu.     Median       Mean    3rd Qu.       Max. 
## -3.119e-02 -3.000e-03  2.721e-05 -2.488e-05  3.577e-03  1.538e-02

Transformation and Stationarity Assessment

d <- ndiffs(log_calls)   # Suggested non-seasonal differencing
D <- nsdiffs(log_calls)  # Suggested seasonal differencing
cat("Suggested: d =", d, ", D =", D, "\n")
## Suggested: d = 1 , D = 1
#first apply seasonal differencing
if (D > 0) {
  calls_seasdiff <- diff(log_calls, lag = frequency(log_calls), differences = D)
} else {
  calls_seasdiff <- log_calls
}

# Plot seasonally differenced series
plot(calls_seasdiff, main = "After Seasonal Differencing", 
     ylab = "Differenced value",
     xlab = "Time")
abline(mean(calls_seasdiff), 0,
       col= 'red')

# ACF of seasonally differenced series
acf(calls_seasdiff, lag.max = 60, main = "ACF after seasonal differencing")

# PACF of seasonally differenced series
pacf(calls_seasdiff, lag.max = 60, main = "PACF after seasonal differencing")

cat("variance of origninal log transformed series", var(log_calls, na.rm=TRUE))
## variance of origninal log transformed series 0.005394091
cat("variance of transformed log transformed series", var(calls_seasdiff, na.rm=TRUE))         # variance of transformed series
## variance of transformed log transformed series 5.813584e-05
# Ljung box test to check stationarity
LB_test <- Box.test(calls_seasdiff, lag = 24, type = "Ljung-Box")
cat("p value of box test",LB_test$p.value )
## p value of box test 4.688461e-06

Model Identification and Diagnostic Checking

# from seasonally differenced ACF and PACF plot suggested AR and MA 
# values are (p=1,2), (q=(1,2)), (P=(0,1)), (Q=(0,1))

# seasonal ARIMA (p,d,q)(P,D,Q)[S] applied on differenced series

# ARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12]
fit_sarima1 <- Arima(log_calls, 
                     order = c(1,1,1),   # non-seasonal ARIMA(p,d,q)       
                     seasonal = list(order = c(0,1,0), period = 12),  #seasonal ARIMA(P,D,Q)[s]
                     include.mean = TRUE)

# ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,0)[12]
fit_sarima2 <- Arima(log_calls, 
                     order = c(1,1,1),          
                     seasonal = list(order = c(1,1,0), period = 12),
                     include.mean = TRUE)

# ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]
fit_sarima3 <- Arima(log_calls, 
                     order = c(1,1,1),           
                     seasonal = list(order = c(1,1,1), period = 12),  
                     include.constant = TRUE)

# ARIMA(2,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]
fit_sarima4 <- Arima(log_calls, 
                     order = c(2,1,1),           
                     seasonal = list(order = c(1,1,1), period = 12),  
                     include.constant = TRUE)

cat("\n=== SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===\n")
## 
## === SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===
summary(fit_sarima1)
## Series: log_calls 
## ARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1      ma1
##       0.4769  -0.9624
## s.e.  0.0959   0.0376
## 
## sigma^2 = 5.256e-05:  log likelihood = 384.19
## AIC=-762.38   AICc=-762.15   BIC=-754.36
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME        RMSE         MAE         MPE       MAPE
## Training set 0.0001852193 0.006781412 0.003722121 0.002135342 0.04627116
##                   MASE       ACF1
## Training set 0.2542041 0.09012426
cat("\n=== SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===\n")
## 
## === SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===
summary(fit_sarima2)
## Series: log_calls 
## ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,0)[12] 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1      ma1     sar1
##       0.4827  -0.9325  -0.3585
## s.e.  0.0994   0.0413   0.0862
## 
## sigma^2 = 4.657e-05:  log likelihood = 391.79
## AIC=-775.58   AICc=-775.19   BIC=-764.89
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME        RMSE         MAE         MPE       MAPE
## Training set 0.0002225009 0.006353323 0.003689291 0.002572858 0.04583286
##                   MASE       ACF1
## Training set 0.2519619 0.09744335
cat("\n=== SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===\n")
## 
## === SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===
summary(fit_sarima3)
## Series: log_calls 
## ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)[12] 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1      ma1     sar1     sma1
##       0.5013  -0.9290  -0.1305  -0.2912
## s.e.  0.0994   0.0411   0.1875   0.1795
## 
## sigma^2 = 4.611e-05:  log likelihood = 392.8
## AIC=-775.61   AICc=-775.01   BIC=-762.24
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME        RMSE         MAE         MPE      MAPE
## Training set 0.0002388294 0.006291338 0.003653322 0.002755247 0.0453779
##                   MASE      ACF1
## Training set 0.2495054 0.1027974
cat("\n=== SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===\n")
## 
## === SARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12] MODEL FITTED ON  DATA ===
summary(fit_sarima4)
## Series: log_calls 
## ARIMA(2,1,1)(1,1,1)[12] 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1      ar2      ma1     sar1     sma1
##       0.5843  -0.2754  -0.8832  -0.1479  -0.2982
## s.e.  0.1031   0.0964   0.0526   0.1821   0.1754
## 
## sigma^2 = 4.384e-05:  log likelihood = 396.6
## AIC=-781.2   AICc=-780.36   BIC=-765.16
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME        RMSE         MAE         MPE       MAPE
## Training set 0.0002011605 0.006104702 0.003548664 0.002286472 0.04409098
##                   MASE        ACF1
## Training set 0.2423577 0.007793724
res1 <- checkresiduals(fit_sarima1)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(1,1,1)(0,1,0)[12]
## Q* = 24.305, df = 22, p-value = 0.3315
## 
## Model df: 2.   Total lags used: 24
res2 <- checkresiduals(fit_sarima2)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,0)[12]
## Q* = 15.178, df = 21, p-value = 0.8139
## 
## Model df: 3.   Total lags used: 24
res3 <- checkresiduals(fit_sarima3)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]
## Q* = 14.746, df = 20, p-value = 0.7908
## 
## Model df: 4.   Total lags used: 24
res4 <- checkresiduals(fit_sarima4)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(2,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]
## Q* = 9.7644, df = 19, p-value = 0.9585
## 
## Model df: 5.   Total lags used: 24

Compare all models

fits <- list(
  SARIMA_111_010 = fit_sarima1,
  SARIMA_111_110 = fit_sarima2,
  SARIMA_111_111 = fit_sarima3,
  SARIMA_211_111 = fit_sarima4
)

# Initialize vectors
model_names <- c()
AIC_vals <- c()
BIC_vals <- c()
AICC_vals <- c()
RMSE_vals <- c()
MAE_vals <- c()
LB_pvals <- c()

for (name in names(fits)) {
  fit <- fits[[name]]
  model_names <- c(model_names, name)
  AIC_vals <- c(AIC_vals, AIC(fit))
  BIC_vals <- c(BIC_vals, BIC(fit))
  AICC_vals <- c(AICC_vals, fit$aicc)
  
  
  # residuals
  res <- residuals(fit)
  RMSE_vals <- c(RMSE_vals, sqrt(mean(res^2)))
  MAE_vals <- c(MAE_vals, mean(abs(res)))
  
  # Ljung-Box test on residuals
  LB_test <- Box.test(res, lag = 24, type = "Ljung-Box")
  LB_pvals <- c(LB_pvals, LB_test$p.value)
}

# Create data frame
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Model = model_names,
  AIC = round(AIC_vals, 3),
  BIC = round(BIC_vals, 3),
  AICC = round(AICC_vals, 3),
  RMSE = round(RMSE_vals, 3),
  MAE = round(MAE_vals, 3),
  Ljung_Box_p = round(LB_pvals, 3)
)

cat("comparison_table", capture.output(comparison_table), sep = "\n")
## comparison_table
##            Model      AIC      BIC     AICC  RMSE   MAE Ljung_Box_p
## 1 SARIMA_111_010 -762.383 -754.365 -762.150 0.007 0.004       0.444
## 2 SARIMA_111_110 -775.579 -764.888 -775.187 0.006 0.004       0.915
## 3 SARIMA_111_111 -775.609 -762.244 -775.015 0.006 0.004       0.928
## 4 SARIMA_211_111 -781.196 -765.159 -780.356 0.006 0.004       0.995
# Plot residuals side by side
p_res1 <- autoplot(fit_sarima1$residuals) + 
  ggtitle("Residuals: SARIMA(1,0,1)(1,1,1)[12]") + 
  theme_minimal()
p_res2 <- autoplot(fit_sarima2$residuals) + 
  ggtitle("Residuals: SARIMA(0,0,1)(1,1,1)[12]") + 
  theme_minimal()
p_res3 <- autoplot(fit_sarima3$residuals) + 
  ggtitle("Residuals: SARIMA(1,1,0)(1,1,1)[12]") + 
  theme_minimal()
p_res4 <- autoplot(fit_sarima4$residuals) + 
  ggtitle("Residuals: SARIMA(1,1,0)(1,1,1)[12]") + 
  theme_minimal()

grid.arrange(p_res1, p_res2, p_res3,p_res4, ncol=2)

Final model

final_fit <- fit_sarima4
res <- residuals(final_fit)

# Residual time plot
plot(res,
     main = "Residuals over time (final model)",
     ylab = "Residuals", xlab = "Time")
abline(h = 0, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Residual ACF 
acf(res, lag.max = 60, main = "ACF of residuals")

pacf(res, lag.max = 60, main = "PACF of residuals")

# Histogram and density plot 
hist(res, breaks = 20, probability = TRUE,
     main = "Histogram of residuals",
     xlab = "Residuals")
lines(density(res, na.rm = TRUE), col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Q–Q plot (normality check)
qqnorm(res, main = "Q–Q plot of residuals")
qqline(res, col = "red", lwd = 2)

# Combined diagnostic 
checkresiduals(final_fit)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(2,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]
## Q* = 9.7644, df = 19, p-value = 0.9585
## 
## Model df: 5.   Total lags used: 24
lb <- Box.test(res,
               lag = 24,
               type = "Ljung-Box",
               fitdf = length(coef(final_fit)))
print(lb)
## 
##  Box-Ljung test
## 
## data:  res
## X-squared = 9.7644, df = 19, p-value = 0.9585
